blocking_findings: Prism security quick scan reports 15 eval-exec findings on re.compile() calls in louke/keeper.py, louke/_tools/check_acs.py, louke/_tools/check_assertions.py, louke/_tools/ci_scan.py. These are FALSE POSITIVES: \bcompile\s*\( pattern matches all re.compile() calls which are static string literal regex compilations, not eval/exec. Pre-existing scanner design issue (louke/_security.py pattern \bcompile\s*\() not introduced by v0.11-001-web-ide. Master user (zillionare) has reviewed and accepted this risk in this session. Same code patterns used by all Louke scanner tools (Prism's own pattern library calls re.compile). Recommend follow-up issue (#111 or similar) to fix _security.py scanner pattern; out of scope for v0.11 web ide.
accepted_risks: eval-exec false-positive on re.compile() — 15 occurrences in keeper.py:116 (RE_ISSUE_RE), louke/_tools/check_acs.py:17-21 (RE_AC_ANCHOR/RE_FR_HEADING/RE_AC_HEADING/RE_AC_COLON/RE_AC_REF), louke/_tools/check_assertions.py:26-58 (RE_AC_REF/RE_ISSUE_OR_URL/FAKE-001..008 patterns). All static regex compilations with literal patterns, no user input. Louke _security.py pattern \bcompile\s*\( should be \b(?!re\.|__)compile\( or scoped to module-level only.

metadata.approved_by: zillionare (Master user via Maestro session 2026-07-11 21:33 UTC)
